177300B53802.text: Above the pyramid, a vast ship appeared, haloed in yellow light. It settled on the summit.<lf>Rams stepped down from its deck—coreless. They tore green shards from niches and altars, then sealed the crystals in dampening caskets. And they flew away, leaving only dust and empty nests at the top.

54391F307A09.text: At night, I heard someone counting my steps. The number did not match. Either I lost a part of myself, or something is walking beside me and imitating me better than I can.

F72044233065.text: At the Forum, the Mortivars promised ‘order.’ By evening, my brother was gone. We found him two days later—standing in their ranks, silent, as if waiting for the next command.

A18A8B7AEE11.text: At the Forum, the Steamers spoke politely, as if it would change anything. We spoke politely, too. And then at night we sharpened bone on stone.

A67B26776819.text: At the crossroads, there’s a sign: ‘Don’t raise a weapon if you want to live.’ It isn’t our sign. Our warnings aren’t that… certain.

7788387724E4.text: Back home, no one sleeps in a single house anymore. We sleep in threes, in fours—spread out. Because if they come, I don’t want them to take all of us at once.

6964F8E6AA64.text: Do not let the wounded from the east inside. Not because they are evil, but because if blue light has entered the bone, it is not the wounded one who returns—only something that remembers the change.

85876D65666A.text: Don’t ask whether Spirit is good. Ask whether you are ready to change.

2275974F868E.text: During a patrol, we encountered an Ent. He did not attack. He simply walked past. One of ours reached for a weapon, and another struck him in the shoulder: ‘Leave it. That is not a target.’

DF6AAFE43647.text: For the first time, I saw a Blaze Crystal by day. It did not sing. It stood like an eye that only counts. And that was worse than silence.

1F492630FB3E.text: Force teaches: everything can be assembled. Death teaches: not everything should be.

523A5163C2F7.text: Forum? For a moment, it truly looked like the world could come to an agreement. And you know what—it’s good to remember it was ever possible at all.

4947B57A9E35.text: From above, streaks of light poured down—green, blue, and gold—like wounds that began to glow. The green fell upon our forests and seeped into the earth. The blue and the gold drifted beyond the horizon, where sight cannot reach.<lf>This event was named The Sowing—because something had been planted, though no one knew what, or why. In its memory, the Eldest carved a sign. Not as a warning. Not as a prayer. But as a remembrance of the moment when the sky first gave something back to the world.

D7225388A62A.text: From small blocks of stone, servants were hewn for heavy labor—the Gronites. They raise great structures: stairs, terraces, and ziggurats, and after storms, they repair cracks, sinkholes, and landslides.<lf>Spirit breathed into them motion and endurance—not life, but obedient existence.

057B09206F87.text: I saw Ent Island high above us, and in its bark I glimpsed a beating heart. We did not pray. We learned to be silent, so we wouldn’t disturb it.

752C70761910.text: I saw a Mortivar once. He stopped by a fence and touched a plank, as if testing the wood. For a moment, he looked… ordinary. Then he walked away, leaving a crack in the plank, like frost had bitten it.

262E10200D30.text: I saw their unit on the road: they walked in step, with no breath, no sign of being tired. When one fell, another picked him up like a bag, and they kept going. Like it was normal.

6F8325951615.text: I saw them after the battle, picking things up from the field like reapers. Not loot, not gold—only helmets, bones, and armor. As if war were a mine.

4D99545BFD2E.text: I was carrying water from the river when they passed me in formation. One turned his head and said, ‘Move.’ It didn’t sound like a threat—it sounded like an order that works even without shouting.

B187A963F036.text: If a tree’s heart is beating, do not stand in its way. Stand to the side and listen to whether the numbers are not in conflict with the leaves.

2345CCA00C5D.text: If you meet a Mortivar and he speaks calmly, run faster. The worst ones are those who still remember words—and use them as bait.

B94E7E80CE37.text: Immortality is not a gift. It is only a refusal to accept the end.

54A0000CA298.text: In the Archive hangs a map of Veltharia from before the fracture. No one updates it. That is our punishment: to remember what ‘one’ looked like. The Grimverds sing so the world may be whole again, the Steamers count so it may be stable, and we keep proof that both methods have already failed once.

D60C2532DA65.text: In the Archive, there is a list of ‘post-Change nonconformities.’ They added me yesterday. Reason: ‘emotional response to rain.’ Wonderful. As if they had better criteria than the weather.

B466998FE4E2.text: In the Rottenbiome, we found the Steamers’ spiders. They did not attack immediately. They stood and watched, as if counting how many of us there were. That felt somehow worse than charging.

1CC98E1B7873.text: In the caravan, we have a new rule: no wounds in plain sight. Not because we’re ashamed—only because they look at wounds differently than we do.

EA4063771A05.text: In the chronicles, we look like order. Funny. Yesterday, three legionaries forgot their names, and we had to call them by the numbers.

F353BA2BB609.text: In the ruins of the Rottenbiome, we found a Steamer cassette marked OFFLINE ONLY. We did not open it. In the Archive, they teach: a yellow heart does not lie—it simply does not know mercy.

FDA79FF3A8FF.text: In the ruins the Yellows left behind, we found their steel casing. Empty. One of the young ones wanted to take a piece as a keepsake. A veteran struck him: ‘Don’t carry their war with you.’

D74D09EEA444.text: In the tavern, they keep saying Nyxthor has a list of names. If yours is on it, you don’t have to sign anything anymore—you already belong to them.

8B300BA5A518.text: In the village, we stopped counting the dead. We started counting those it’s impossible to bury.

001AE1F4D0CC.text: In the zone of the rotten biome, we do not sing an open song. Only short notes, in the shadows—like passing beside a sleeping predator.

32CB13908EFB.text: Iron does not hear the song, but it hears the vibration.

7375D366BC26.text: My helmet reflects a face I do not have. I look into the metal and see a man. I write this down, because tomorrow I may see only a tool.

C56E5A3517ED.text: My mother used to say: ‘From plague you run, from soldiers you hide.’ From the Mortivars, you can do neither—because they don’t look for the living. They look for material.

371113CD50F9.text: NYXTHOR - Scholar of the Force Crystal.<lf>The one who learned to wield Force like a blade: steady, without hesitation, and without retreat. In His hands, the blue ceased to be chance—it became a tool and a mark of authority.<lf>Let His name be a seal of knowledge that does not fade.

2EA0984F8EDC.text: Nyxthor arrived, made his rounds, and did not even pretend to recognize us. Asked about an end, he said: ‘When you stop breaking down.’

663D85DACB5F.text: Nyxthor made us stand without moving until the blue stopped trembling in our bones. It lasted all night. In the morning, he said only: ‘Good. You are not reverting.’

94E4DC353147.text: Nyxthor promised the Change would take the pain away. It did… but it left an itch. It itches where my heart used to be. As if someone is still knocking there.

AFFBB0275863.text: Nyxthor used to say: ‘Force brings order.’ Today I see that Force replaces. Order without end is only a cage without a door.

B24E534EAAC6.text: On an old battlefield, I found a medal. Ordinary, human. I don’t know who it belonged to, but it suddenly reminded me what pride looks like in someone’s eyes—and I hate that memory.

DC6E223416BB.text: On the board after the battle, one sentence was added by a different hand: ‘If green and blue begin to dance, flee from the edge of the world.’

AB5037C76140.text: On the island with the anchor-tree, I saw a heart inside the trunk. It did not beat. It circled. And I understood that the world is trying to stitch itself back together, though we tore it open.

4CF33EB695D4.text: On the northern terraces, we found metal traces. They weren’t old—only abandoned. We carved a sign: ‘Iron returns’—so no one would think the forest had forgotten.

72E300FF3C7D.text: On the pedestal, a carving reads:<cf>"In honor of Nyxthor, Lord of Wisdom and Keeper of Ancient Truths, whose name will never be forgotten."

B35841542ADF.text: On the pedestal, a carving reads:<cf>“Glory to the warriors who have fought and defended the innocent.”

F61F062CA4C8.text: On the pedestal, a carving reads:<cf>“Glory to the warriors who have fought and defended the innocent.”

F2C338AFBC2D.text: On the pedestal, a carving reads:<cf>“In memory of King Edmar, the kingdom’s shield and a light to his people.”

2E584B50FA50.text: On the pedestal, a defaced carving reads:<cf>"In honor of Nyxthor, Lord of Wisdom…"—the rest has been violently scratched out. Crude letters have been carved over it:<cf>"Eternal Tyrant, who condemned us to endless suffering."

B01C7CCB9DC4.text: Once, we feared dying. Now we fear existing without end. That is the difference between a human and a Mortivar.

30CFD9C9791D.text: One of the recruits asked me if a person still dreams after the Change. I said, ‘No.’ I lied. I dream only in my dreams; I am always marching.

AD649E6862A5.text: Someone said the Mortivars take only warriors. A lie. They took the blacksmith because he had strong hands. They took the scribe because he could write.

EE5DE8166EB5.text: Sometimes I catch myself counting days. And days no longer mean anything. Yet in my head, there is still a man waiting for the end of a shift.

15B943CE11FF.text: Stone remembers better than an ear. If you want a song to endure, sing into stone, not into the crowd.

9ABC27720BD6.text: THE MONARCH, AETERNUS VORTALIS - The Eternal Lord of Power.<lf>The one who closed the power of the crystals within His hand and gave it to none. The one who mastered their radiance, bound it with law, and made it a shield for His reign.<lf>Let the memory endure of the Monarch who did not ask for power—he simply raised it.

231E3F0966D7.text: The Archive has a new rule: ‘Do not mention your former life in the presence of recruits.’ Pity. The recruits ask anyway. Most often: ‘Do you remember the taste of bread?’

AA3D1ABE5CC5.text: The Change took my breath, but it did not take my habits. I still adjust my belt, though I cannot feel the weight of my belly. Stupid. Real.

D8D3CE052055.text: The Change was not a ritual. It was a patch applied to living material. Nyxthor entered it in the Archive as an ‘improvement.’ In the margin, we added: ‘irreversible.’

D4492F4DB634.text: The Grimverds came to our settlement and sprinkled a handful of green dust across the threshold. They said, ‘It won’t stop them. But it will remind you when to run.’

1AEC1FD6F8A1.text: The Grimverds sing when they bury the fallen. We do not sing. Where we have no sarcophagus, we weigh them down with stone. Not out of contempt, only because we know how easily something can rise.

3DECFD117DBF.text: The Grimverds warned us with a song I did not understand—but I understood the fear in their eyes. They said only: ‘They are coming—those who leave no shadows.’

BBB8100BC76C.text: The Gronites gathered the largest blocks and assembled from them a body that rose higher than the temple gates—a colossus with a stone sword. A heart made of Spirit was embedded inside him, woven into a single breath, able to sustain movement in such a huge form.<cf>At the foot of the Ziggurat, they raised a throne for him, so he might stand guard—and by his very presence shatter the courage of his foes.

B231DF9025A6.text: The Gronites, made for building, took their stand on the stairs and at the temple entrances, throwing off every intruder as though the stone itself resisted all outsiders. The Grimverds watched over them and, with a heavy heart, reached for the Spirit Crystal, giving the Gronites the ability to defend.<lf>And as the attack dragged on, the Gronites did not give way even a single step, enduring like a wall of stone.

BF08005A1B6D.text: The Mortivars came for Spirit and said it was ‘material.’ We answered: ‘It is a voice.’

838686C5091E.text: The Mortivars say, ‘Force brings order.’ We say, ‘Spirit breathes.’

0C665F53D242.text: The Steamers left behind smoke and pipes. The Ents leave silence and traces of roots. Both are signs that the world tried to heal itself.

7CC22D7C34F2.text: The Steamers once came and took the Blaze Crystal from the desert. The Mortivars came and took people from the road. I prefer the ones from the sky—at least they don’t pretend it’s for our own good.

2C78657C0A37.text: The first Ent I ever saw did not look at us. He looked into the ground, as if reading a map beneath the roots. We did not offer him tribute. We left him space—because he was a guardian of balance, not a god.

CB2F9CBF343C.text: The old priest says it’s a punishment. I say it’s a method. Nyxthor didn’t make monsters by accident—he made an army that doesn’t ask whether it has the right.

7967CC7D0A02.text: There are no heroes. Only those who have not seen the price yet.

AEA3EDCF10D9.text: They say the Change does not hurt. A lie. It hurts everyone around because they have to watch someone vanish, and yet still stand right in front of them.

A3510128A6EF.text: Those who sing to rule—snap the strings. Those who sing to listen—awaken the roots.

4E02275E27F0.text: Today, they repaired my ribs after the battle. They used someone else’s parts. They fit. Only now the left side of my body ‘remembers’ a different war than the right one.

2D146B16A852.text: Veltharia is no longer one whole land, but now every home has a view. Funny how the world can give something back in return.

7B1B139183A6.text: War does not end after the battle. It ends only when you stop recognizing yourself.

9117C6D9C2E8.text: We found a machine in the ruins with a yellow heart. It stood still, but its shadow was alert. We did not approach. Not because we were afraid, only because we remembered the Forum.

E9A6BA293CE6.text: We found their tracks in the mud. They were even, as if stamped with a seal. There was no hurry in them. It is always haste that betrays the living.

4DF49BABAF25.text: When I was alive, I feared the sword. Now I fear the sword will be too light. Force cannot tolerate weakness—even the kind no one can see.

C8ACAAC3952E.text: When Veltharia began to tear apart, our song could not stitch it back together. Then we saw the truth: it is not we who hold the islands—it is the trees with hearts that hold us.<lf>And we carved into stone: We are not masters of the forest. We are its burden.

F6172A6013D3.text: When the Yellows came from the sky, they asked for neither water nor fire. They asked for silence—because in silence it is easier to hear where Blaze sleeps in the earth.

EEDFC961A29D.text: When the enemies came to the steps of the ziggurat, the Gronites silently assembled a Golem’s body from stone. The Grimverds carved the Defender’s sigils on its chest, breaking an ancient law—Spirit’s radiance was meant to rouse endurance, not wrath.<lf>The Golem took its place on the stairs, and for the first time, Stone rose against those who had come for what was never meant for them.

8304DAB46E13.text: When the song does not answer, do not sing louder. It isn’t your voice that’s too weak—the question is wrong.

Early Access takes place on an unlimited map, giving you complete freedom to explore and experience the atmosphere of the world we are building.<cf><cf>Please note that some minor bugs may occur -<DemoNB>this is still work in progress.</> The co-op mode is still a work in progress as well, and we’re actively testing its stability and functionality. 79223348C462.text: A fair price for good goods.

CF8E1FB09336.text: A market is a market, but if someone pays me in shards again, I’ll give change in stones.

8C005696B00D.text: A stranger? Rare sight in these parts.

9ECA725C20FA.text: At night, you can hear footsteps in the Steamers’ ruins. In the morning, no tracks.

F295E0B66960.text: Better tell me who you are.

32BED4A63F25.text: Check the merchants—often they’ve got something interesting… Makes me wonder where they get it from.

95D4D337A854.text: Come back if you need more.

281735B308C0.text: Don’t ask why my cabbage glows. Ask if you want it cheaper.

EBFD7297788A.text: Don’t trust maps. The islands have moods of their own.

766D3A36E209.text: Ents? Some say holy, some say cursed. I say: better not provoke a tree.

C10713503D71.text: Everything is here. Pick what you want!

8B80FA215B8D.text: Hmm… first time I’ve seen you.

3791D4597ACE.text: I can show you what’s good for the road.

19DE73C9FAD9.text: I don’t have time to talk.

2897D479CCD3.text: I don’t know you… what are you looking for here?

E43CE94D9D4C.text: I don’t need grand stories. I need my roof not to leak.

1E99A8C209C5.text: I don’t trade with Mortivars. They haggle too long. And you might lose your head.

6B5DA4BD4C98.text: I get it—not everyone buys right away.

363EDF8B4E46.text: I have good stuff. Have a look!

FEF895C51FC6.text: I have nothing to do with this!

3E9603CEAC36.text: If it’s not rain, it’s dust. If it rains, it’s mud. If it’s good, it’ll probably end soon.

C4847D08B1CF.text: If you see metal footprints in the sand, turn back. This isn’t a place for the living.

B080FA822D58.text: If you want good quality, you’re in the right place.

28F81976F0E8.text: I’m not saying he lies… I’m just saying he never tells the truth either.

665EB1829E1C.text: I’m telling you, that new one from the neighboring hut… acts quiet, but knows everything first.

0A94AEDF0030.text: I’ve got nothing else to say.

0D374962BD5E.text: Morning, you get up, evening, you lie down, and in between you fight life—and doors that stick.

9A51993F0E39.text: Mortivars sign contracts for a hundred years. And they have the time to enforce them.

480906FDC571.text: My boots are soaked, my mood too, and the work is still piling up.

88E82A904B05.text: My brother went to gather Blaze in the desert.

9DAE8AE7979D.text: My pig looks at me like it knows more than I do. And that worries me.

30E11C471881.text: No pressure. Come back if you change your mind.

C418ED5CF7AF.text: No pressure. Just showing my goods.

5337649ED7E1.text: On this island, everything moves. Even the stones, when you’re not looking.

08810B56797E.text: Safe travels with your new gear!

93F6E3DAC97B.text: Take a look. Maybe you’ll like something!

E9F174D450A0.text: The Grimverds’ ziggurats? Beautiful. But don’t stand on the stairs when they start to sing.

B3D5C8662A4A.text: The Steamers were stealing Spirit from pyramids before anyone knew to call it war.

74975E875D70.text: The pheasant ran onto the roof again. If it falls, at least we’ll have dinner.

CC618857E974.text: The well water is fine—until someone starts telling stories about what they “once” saw in it.

86454BDE1A3A.text: There’s no free miracle. Every crystal takes something in return.

A8A5B0FF5370.text: They say the chapels have catacombs. Haven’t seen anyone go in.

2DF88A3B8D65.text: Think about it and come back later.

F062C3B849B1.text: This will be useful to you.

915244038BBF.text: Today is so windy that my laundry will fly to the next island any minute.

8841D4E2F0BB.text: Today was supposed to be rest. Then life showed up and said, “no.”

BE7635ABD8F7.text: Try it, if you’ve got the courage.

EBF4AF4348AA.text: We don’t argue here. We just talk loud and long.

D3CC47651BE2.text: We don’t get many visitors here.

F78CDFB53697.text: We don’t steal here. The guards keep order.

0AE11FBCA769.text: You don’t “fall” off this island. You “suddenly vanish” if you don’t watch your step.

D8457274DE45.text: A Flying Ship can be used to travel between larger islands. <cf>Flying Ships that you can take control of contain a Flying Ship Core. To take control of a Flying Ship, you need to place and interact with its Cockpit. <cf>Once you place the Cockpit, your Home Point is set to that Ship, meaning that upon death, you will respawn there, and using the Starter Compass will teleport you directly to it. <cf>If the Cockpit is destroyed, the Home Point linked to that Flying Ship will be lost.

CAA60C20112A.text: Altitude in the game affects the biomes you encounter at different heights. As you ascend, enemies become tougher, and the Loot from Dungeons improves. Higher altitudes also unlock better recipes. To reach these elevated areas, you'll need to upgrade your Flying Ship using a Flying Ship Core and with Enhanced Balloons, allowing you to fly higher and access more challenging and rewarding locations.

70E81F815AB9.text: Armor crafting can only be done at the Smithing Station. To craft an Armor piece, you first need to know its recipe. Once you have the recipe, go to the Smithing Station, select the Armor piece you wish to create, and if you have the required materials, press the "Craft" button to create the selected item. <cf><cf>Armor pieces provide Defense, which helps reduce the Damage taken from enemy attacks, and they also have a Durability value.

6651E070BE61.text: As items lose Durability, their effectiveness gradually decreases. When items break completely, they lose all functionality. Damaged or broken Tools, Weapons, and Equipment can be repaired using Repair Kits. These kits can be used anywhere, but they restore less Durability compared to using them at an Anvil. Higher-quality Anvils allow for even more efficient repairs, making them a valuable resource for maintaining your Gear.

99C3D8FC6CF8.text: Balloons are essential for increasing the Flying Ship's altitude, allowing it to rise to greater heights. To use Balloons, they must be connected to a power source, such as an Energy Generator, via Pipes.<cf><cf>Better-quality Balloons enable the Flying Ship to reach even higher altitudes, improving its overall capabilities, as long as the Flying Ship Core is upgraded to support those altitude levels. Make sure to keep your Flying Ship powered to maintain the Balloons' effectiveness and maximize your flying potential.

3741BDC33AEC.text: Balloons increase a Flying Ship's altitude and must be powered via Pipes from a Generator.

42252D1EB57E.text: Basic blocks and roofs can have different shapes while keeping their original properties. Select a block on the hotbar, hold {ShowRadial} to open the Rosette, and use {ItemBarScroll} to choose a shape.

2B9A67709809.text: Block crafting is done using the Block Station. In this station, you can choose which type of Block you want to create. Additionally, you can select different variations of the Block to craft based on your preferences or needs. Simply select the Block and its version, and if you have the required materials, press the "Craft" button to create it.

232AE8AF7CC9.text: Bosses are powerful enemies with unique abilities and resistances. Defeating them grants high Experience and a chance for rare Loot.

8083ED8865B2.text: Bosses are powerful enemies, far stronger than regular mobs. They have unique abilities, attack patterns, and resistances. Defeating them requires proper preparation, a sufficient character level, and Gear.<cf><cf>In return for the challenge, Bosses reward you with significantly more Experience and a chance to obtain rare and valuable items.

0940CB30C470.text: Bows are two-handed Weapons that require Arrows to function. The bow determines the base Damage, while the Arrow Type provides additional Effects.<cf><cf>To fire an Arrow, hold {PrimaryAction} to draw the bow—the longer you hold it, the more powerful the shot, increasing both range and Damage. Pressing {SecondaryAction} while drawing cancels the shot.<cf><cf>If you have multiple types of Arrows in your Inventory, you can switch between them while holding a bow in your Hotbar. Hold {ShowRadial} to open the Arrow selection wheel and use the {ItemBarScroll} to cycle through available Arrow Types.

EF4301D62D67.text: Bows are two-handed Weapons that use Arrows. Hold {PrimaryAction} to draw and release to shoot. Hold {ShowRadial} to select Arrow Type and use {ItemBarScroll} to switch between them. {SecondaryAction} cancels the shot.

B99E8830E312.text: Broken items lose functionality. Repair Kits restore Durability anywhere, but Anvils (especially higher-quality ones) repair more efficiently.

EDB5BADD2F00.text: Build a Flying Ship by crafting Cockpit, Engine, Balloon, and Energy Generator. Unlock their recipes and gather materials from shipwrecks located on larger islands.

ADC4DB7BB768.text: Coins are the main currency for trading with merchants who buy and sell items. Different merchants offer different prices and goods and are usually found in villages.

A4A1E7E9B6C1.text: Combat revolves around the use of Weapons, each with its own Damage value. Every attack consumes Stamina and reduces the Durability of the Weapon. A single {PrimaryAction} press performs a quick attack, while repeatedly pressing executes a fast attack combo. Holding {PrimaryAction} charges a strong attack, and moving in one of the four basic directions while holding {PrimaryAction} triggers a directional strong attack suited to the movement. {SecondaryAction} is used to block incoming attacks. Each attack has a chance to deal Critical Damage, with increased odds when striking an enemy from behind or attacking while hidden.

D5FD0FF28601.text: Combat uses Weapons that consume Stamina.<cf>Press {PrimaryAction} for quick attacks,<cf>Hold {PrimaryAction} for a strong attack,<cf>Use {SecondaryAction} to block.

6FE9FE0E3F2B.text: Craft Armor at the Smithing Station using known recipes and required materials. Armor provides Defense and has Durability.

F3B77A40949F.text: Craft Weapons at the Smithing Station using known recipes and required materials.

2420C6EF4D6F.text: During your adventures, you may encounter elite and legendary versions of creatures that possess powers far beyond their normal counterparts. These enemies are much more challenging to defeat, but in return, they drop rarer items and grant significantly higher amounts of Coins and Experience.

8526DED4A484.text: Dying comes with certain penalties. Upon death, you will respawn at your last set Home Point, such as a claimed Flying Ship. You will return with a reduced amount of Health, scaled proportionally to your character's level. Additionally, any non-permanent buffs will be lost.<cf><cf>When you die, you leave behind a Death Orb that temporarily stores the items you dropped. Only items from specific categories, such as Treasure and Resources, are transferred into the Orb. The Death Orb persists for a limited time before disappearing and is marked by an icon, making it easier to locate and recover your belongings.

6DEDDB83BE20.text: Dying respawns you at your last Home Point. You will return with reduced Health scaled to your character's level, and a Death Orb will appear at the place of your death, temporarily holding the lost items. The Orb is time-limited and marked by an icon.

5B872FD8B458.text: Elite and legendary creatures are tougher than normal enemies but drop rarer items and grant more coins and Experience.

BF61D7A7469C.text: Engines control the speed and direction of your Flying Ship. They need to be connected to a power source, such as an Energy Generator, via Pipes to function properly. The quality and number of Engines determine how quickly your Flying Ship accelerates, as long as the Flying Ship Core is upgraded to support those Speed Levels.

DB5555685D4D.text: Engines control your Flying Ship’s speed and direction, connect them to a power source via Pipes to function properly.

5E65CA54A9AF.text: Equip the Glider in its Slot and press {Jump} while in the air to glide.<cf>Dive/Ascend/Turn – {Move},<cf>Retract – {Jump},<cf><cf>Collisions reduce Glider Durability.

55292A6342C6.text: Every basic Block and Roof can be placed in a variety of shapes. Blocks that are modified in shape still retain all the properties of their original form, meaning they function in the same way as the standard Block. <cf><cf>To change shape, choose Block/Roof on the Hotbar, then open Rosette: Hold {ShowRadial}<cf>Use {ItemBarScroll} to select shape.

BB1DD0039CA8.text: Every character has a level that increases as the Experience bar is filled. When leveling up, your base Health increases, and you earn a Skill Point that can be spent in the Skill Tree.<cf><cf>Experience is gained by defeating enemies, with tougher opponents granting more Experience. The Skill Tree is divided into three main branches: Warrior, Arcanist, and Engineer. To unlock a skill, you must have an available skill point and have previously unlocked any required preceding skills. Some abilities can be upgraded multiple times using additional skill points, enhancing their effectiveness.

DD6AC4862C63.text: Falling from a great height can cause Damage upon landing. The higher the fall, the more Damage received. If the fall is too extreme, it can even result in Death. Be cautious when navigating high places, and consider using a Glider or other means to break your fall.

97AED677EE5F.text: Falling from heights causes Damage, increasing with fall distance. Extreme falls can be fatal—use a Glider or other means to reduce impact.

B8C8003B6A94.text: Gain XP by defeating enemies to level up and earn Skill Points. Spend them in the Warrior, Arcanist, or Engineer tree to unlock or upgrade abilities.

93E49579D5EC.text: Generators produce a fixed amount of Energy using Fuel. Insert Fuel and press “Start” to activate the Generator. It will supply power to connected devices via Pipes up to its Energy Capacity Limit.

47172925B328.text: Glasthyr Monoliths are Magical Objects that grant unique, temporary buffs to you when you interact with them. These buffs can provide powerful advantages for a limited time.<cf><cf>Monoliths can also be destroyed, yielding Glasthyr, a special resource used for upgrading items.

300D66862521.text: Glasthyr Monoliths grant temporary buffs when activated. They can also be destroyed to obtain Glasthyr for item upgrades.

C00D184DF588.text: Grow crops using a Pot or Incubator, correct Soil, a seedling, and a watering item. Keep the Soil watered to continue growth. Wrong Soil, incorrect altitude, or lack of Water stops growth and causes rotting. Incubators ignore altitude restrictions.

230B6BA93855.text: Handy Crafting allows the crafting of basic items only. Learned recipes don’t unlock new options. Advanced items require a Crafting Station or other specialized stations.

7610752BA10B.text: Higher altitude affects biomes, tougher enemies, better Dungeon Loot, and unlocks improved recipes. Upgrade your Ship and use Enhanced Balloons to reach elevated areas.

B451943A8C69.text: In Handy Crafting, you can craft only basic items. To craft an item, simply choose the item you want to create. If you have the required materials, press "Craft", and you will receive the item.<cf><cf>Recipes that you have learned do not unlock new crafting options in Handy Crafting. To craft more advanced items, you must build a Crafting Station or other specialized Crafting Stations.

51A5B4094A45.text: In Sandbox Mode, you have complete freedom to explore, build, and experiment without limitations.<cf>You are immortal, your actions do not consume Stamina, and you can freely fly, making movement effortless across the world.<cf><cf>Fly On – Double-press {Jump}<cf>Fly Off – Double-press {Jump} when flying<cf>Fly Up/Down – {FlyUpDown} when flying<cf><cf>A special Inventory provides unlimited access to all items, objects, and mobs, categorized into tabs for easy navigation: <cf><cf>All / Blocks / Objects / Plants / Specials / Miscellaneous / Potions & Food / Armors / Weapons / Tools / Recipes / Mobs.

F5D97DD2188B.text: Locked Chests contain better Loot and require a Key or sufficient Lockpicking skills to open. Hold a Key or Lockpick in hand and interact with the Chest to unlock it.

1F55CE10DBEC.text: Pipes transfer Energy from Generators to devices like Engines and Balloons. They link automatically to adjacent Pipes and light up when connected to a power source.

EC762C20691A.text: Pipes transfer Energy to devices and light up when connected, they link automatically to adjacent Pipes.

BE3F46CE605C.text: Press {InteractionAction} to interact with the Cockpit:<cf><cf>Speed/Turn - {Move}<cf>Adjust Height - {FlyUpDown}<cf>Brake - {ForceStopLever}<cf>Switch perspectives - {SwitchCamera}<cf><cf>Press {QuitCockpit} to exit

ECF7DEBD4ACB.text: Recipes allow you to unlock the ability to craft various items, Weapons, Armor, Food, and Objects. Using a recipe adds an entry to your Compendium, providing details on the required materials and the Crafting Station needed. Once learned, a recipe is permanently unlocked. Higher-tier recipes are rarer and can be found on higher-floating islands.

73A01777733C.text: Recipes unlock crafting for items, Weapons, Armor, and more. Learning a recipe adds it to your Compendium permanently. Higher-tier recipes are rarer and found on higher islands.

2485749EE82B.text: Runes are magical items used to infuse Equipment with special effects. Each rune has its own unique ability and exists in five tiers, with higher tiers granting stronger effects. To apply a rune, hold it in your hand and use it to open the Rune Infusion interface. There, you'll see a list of compatible items from your Inventory that can receive the rune's effect. Applying a rune also requires a certain amount of Force Crystal Dust. Each item can hold only one rune effect. Once the target item is selected, the rune and dust are consumed, and the Equipment becomes infused with the rune's power.<cf><cf>Infused items can also be placed into a Processing Station to extract a rune with a weaker effect than the one originally infused into the item, allowing partial recovery of rune power.

2642BA1DDE67.text: Runes infuse Equipment with magical effects. Hold a rune to open the infusion menu, choose a compatible item, and spend Force Crystal Dust to apply it. Each item can hold one special effect.

9FB4C550E617.text: Sandbox Mode gives full freedom: immortality, no Stamina use, and flying enabled. Access all items via a special Inventory organized into categories for easy building and experimenting.

8BC7CA700CD3.text: Shields can be equipped in the Shield Slot and carried in the off-hand. When equipped, they add their Defense value to your total Defense and replace Weapon blocking with Shield blocking. Blocking with a Shield consumes Shield Durability instead of Weapon Durability.<cf><cf>To parry, press {PrimaryAction} when blocking at the moment an enemy attack lands—this staggers the enemy, giving you a short window to strike back.

8D6F98EB5301.text: Shields go in the off-hand, add Defense, and replace Weapon blocking. Press {PrimaryAction} when blocking at the moment of an enemy attack to parry, staggering them, and creating an opening for a counterattack.

88B55DBDC6D2.text: Special Tools are permanent items without Durability. They can’t be dropped and are always available. <cf><cf>Use {ItemBarCompass} to select a Tool.<cf>Hold {ShowRadial} to open the item wheel.

75DFB8DA8299.text: Special Tools are unique items that, once unlocked, are automatically added to the Special Tools Slot. <cf>Unlike regular items, they never wear out, cannot be dropped, and are always available to you. <cf>Examples of Special Tools include the Starter Compass and the Spyglass. <cf>Some Special Tools can be upgraded through unlocked skills.<cf><cf>Choose Special Tools Slot – {ItemBarCompass}<cf>Open Chosen Item Rosette – Hold {ShowRadial}

855C87025E0A.text: Staffs and Spellbooks consume power when casting spells. Use a Spirit Recharge Station with Spirit Dust to restore their power.

524587082505.text: Staffs and Spellbooks used for casting spells have a power value that is consumed when spells are cast. To restore this power, you need a Spirit Recharge Station and Spirit Dust. Open the Station interface, place the partially depleted Staff or Spellbook into the appropriate Slot, and insert Spirit Dust into the Fuel Slot. Then press "Start" to begin the recharging process. After a short time, if enough Spirit Dust is provided, the item will be fully recharged with magical power.

823CEB9778A6.text: Steamer Vaults are ancient structures that can only be accessed using a Steamer Vault Key or a special metal extraction tool. Inside, you can find rare recipes for unique Steamer items. The Steamer Vault Key can be found in Laboratory Rooms in Mortivar Dungeons.

88E70F7016A7.text: Steamer Vaults require a Steamer Vault Key or extraction Tool. Inside are rare recipes for unique Steamer items. Keys are found in Laboratory Rooms in Mortivar Dungeons.

37720042E2AA.text: Step into the Launcher with a Crystal of Blaze to be propelled into the air. Open your Glider to navigate between floating islands. One crystal is consumed per use.

4BB54F3D13DF.text: Take control of a Flying Ship by placing and interacting with its Cockpit. Your Home Point is set to the Flying Ship, and the Starter Compass teleports you there.

D3A7FDF23E72.text: The Carpenter Station is used to craft all kinds of furniture and Decorative Objects, such as Chests, Seating, Tables, and Light Sources. Open the Station interface, select the item you want to create, and, if you have the required materials, press the "Craft" button to obtain the Object.<cf>

A0EF9C5F0267.text: The Character Inventory consists of two sections: the left side, which contains your Inventory Slots, and the right side, which displays your currently Equipped items. Your Inventory holds all items you currently possess, with Hotbar Slots located at the bottom for quick access. The Equipment section includes dedicated Slots for Armor (Helmet, Chestplate, Gloves, and Boots), a Ring, an Amulet, a Shield, and a Glider.

2A48C64788A7.text: The Character Inventory shows your items on the left and equipped Gear on the right. Hotbar Slots allow quick access, and Equipment Slots include Armor, Ring, Amulet, Shield, and Glider.

510767105033.text: The Cooking Station is used to prepare meals. To begin cooking, open the Cooking Station interface, place a Fuel item into the Fuel Slot, and then select the recipe you want to create. If you have the required ingredients, press the "Start Cooking" button to begin the process. Once the dish is ready, steam will rise from the cooking pot, indicating that the meal is ready for collection. Meals are the primary way to heal yourself and provide small positive effects.

F60ECCC638D0.text: The Crafting Station is used to craft basic items and some items that are learned through collecting recipes. To craft an item, simply choose the item you want to create. If you have the required materials, press "Craft", and you will receive the item.<cf><cf>To craft specialized items such as Weapons, Armor, Mechanisms, and other advanced Objects, you will need to use specialized Crafting Stations.

D88A7B184327.text: The Fabricator crafts Steamer items including advanced Weapons, Armor, and devices like the Glider.

A0385C0D6161.text: The Fabricator is a special Crafting Station used to create items related to Steamers, including their advanced Weapons, Armor, and devices. One example of an item you can craft here is the Glider.<cf><cf>To craft an item in the Fabricator, select a recipe you've already learned. If you have all the required materials, press the "Craft" button to create the item.

5F8930F4AA43.text: The Flying Ship Core is a special, indestructible Object found on Flying Ships that allows you to upgrade your Flying Ship’s overall performance parameters. You can improve three key attributes separately: <cf><cf>- the maximum speed your Ship can reach <cf>- the maximum altitude it can ascend to <cf>- and the Ship’s building boundary<cf><cf>Upgrading these parameters only increases your Ship’s potential—to actually reach higher speeds or altitudes, you still need to build and power the appropriate mechanisms, such as Engines and Balloons.<cf><cf>To upgrade a parameter, open the Flying Ship Core interface, select the desired upgrade, and, if you have the required materials, press “Upgrade”.<cf><cf>Flying Ship Cores emit a straight white beacon of light into the sky, marking the Ship’s location.

B1AF13AF4400.text: The Flying Ship Core lets you upgrade your Ship's speed, altitude, and build area. Open its interface, select an upgrade, and press "Upgrade" if you have the materials.

8F83CADB67C3.text: The Furnace crafts Ingots from resources using Fuel. Place Fuel, select an Ingot, and press "Craft" to process the materials.

BB40123F9EB3.text: The Furnace is used to craft various types of Ingots from extracted resources. These Ingots are essential for crafting advanced Mechanisms and upgrading your Equipment. To use the Furnace, open its interface, place a Fuel item into the Fuel Slot, then select the Ingot you want to craft. If you have the required materials, press "Craft", and the Furnace will begin processing the resources into the selected Ingot.

C98CB90570A0.text: The Glider is a special item that allows you to glide through the skies. <cf>To use it, equip the Glider in the dedicated Glider Slot in your Inventory. <cf>Once equipped, press {Jump} while in the air to deploy it and begin gliding.<cf><cf>Use {Move} to dive and gain speed, or pull up to slow down. You can also use it to steer the Glider. <cf>Retract the glider – {Jump}<cf><cf>The Glider has its own Durability, which decreases when you collide with objects while flying.

4BA07C1B1DDF.text: The Launcher is a powerful device that propels players high into the air, allowing them to deploy their Glider and travel between floating islands. To use it, simply step inside with a Crystal of Blaze in your Inventory and interact to activate. The device will consume the Crystal, close around you, and after a short delay, launch you skyward. Once airborne, you can open your Glider and navigate freely. The Launcher can only be activated from within and requires one Crystal of Blaze per use. These devices are found in Steamer race structures, making them essential Tools for exploration and vertical movement.

71F788471A8E.text: The Processing Station breaks items into some of their original components. Connect it to power, place items in the Slots, and press "Start" to process them one at a time.

F8B05AEA58C6.text: The Processing Station is used to break down items into some of the components they were originally crafted from. To use the Processing Station, it must first be connected and powered, for example, by linking it to a Generator via Pipes.<cf><cf>Once the Station is connected and powered, open its interface, place the items into the processing Slots, and press "Start" to begin processing. Items are processed one at a time, so processing a large number of items may take some time.

30799382BB79.text: The Refinery extracts Liquids from Wet Blocks. Place it above a deposit, connect to power via Pipes, select the Liquid, and press "Start" to collect it.

7412492CC8DA.text: The Refinery is used to extract Liquids such as Water, Fuel, or Magical Fluids from special Wet Blocks. To use the Refinery, first locate a Wet Block deposit and place the Refinery above it. Next, connect a power source, such as an Energy Generator, to the Refinery using Pipes to supply it with Energy.<cf><cf>Once everything is set up, open the Refinery interface, select the Liquid available in range of the Refinery (the displayed quantity indicates the size of the deposit), and press "Start" to begin extracting the Liquid. After extraction, the collected Liquid can be collected from the Refinery.

97F3BE73D62C.text: The Rune Crafting Station is used to combine Rune Fragments and Runes into stronger forms. Placing Runalit and five Rune Fragments of the same type into the station will create one complete Rune. In turn, combining five identical Runes will produce an upgraded Rune with greater power. To craft, open the station's interface, select the desired Rune, and if you have the required components, press Craft to complete the process.<cf><cf>Runes are used to apply Special Effects to Weapons and Chestplates, enhancing their power and granting unique abilities.

C28852EC3DB7.text: The Spyglass is a Special Tool that helps you discover interesting properties of nearby islands.<cf>Hold {SecondaryAction} to look through it and use {SkyGlassZoom} to zoom in and out.<cf>While aiming at an island, its distance will be displayed at the top of the lens, along with an indicator showing whether the island has already been visited.<cf>If the island contains a Dungeon, a glowing Dungeon icon will appear on the right side of the screen.

822C2844013A.text: The Spyglass reveals island details. Hold {SecondaryAction} to look and use {SkyGlassZoom} to zoom, making it easier to spot structures.

D4328CEC639B.text: The Starter Compass always points to your Home. Select it with {ItemBarCompass}, then hold {SecondaryAction} to teleport back to your Home Point after a short delay.

9733B65DA574.text: The Starter Compass is one of the Special Tools and always shows the direction to your Home Point. Choose Special Tools Slot – {ItemBarCompass}. When holding the Compass in your hand, hold {SecondaryAction} for a few seconds to teleport back to your Home Point after a short delay.

B4D58040A9C7.text: The upgraded item will have improved stats, making it stronger or more efficient than its previous version. At certain Upgrade Levels, there's a chance that the upgrade will not only increase the item's base stats but also grant an additional modifier or enhance an existing one.<cf><cf>The chance of success is displayed once the item is placed in the Upgrade Station. The Upgrade Station always upgrades an item by one level at a time, with a maximum Upgrade Level of 10. As the Upgrade Level increases, more Crystals of Force and Resources are required for the process.

2681D0D92884.text: To create Potions, you need an Alchemy Station, Magical Liquid, and Alchemy Ingredients. A basic Potion with a specific Effect is crafted by placing three Ingredients with the same magical aspect into the Alchemy Station. More advanced Alchemy Stations allow for the mixing of additional Ingredients, enabling the creation of stronger Potion variants if the extra Ingredients share the same magical aspect.<cf><cf>If the selected Ingredients contain multiple shared magical aspects, the Potion will gain an Effect for each aspect that appears at least three times among the Ingredients. This allows for the creation of Potions with multiple Effects, depending on the combination of Ingredients used.

2DB86713FFF5.text: To grow crops, you need a Pot or Incubator, a seedling, the correct Soil, and a watering item. Start by placing the appropriate Soil in the Pot or Incubator, then insert a seedling that can grow in that specific Soil. You can check which Soil a seedling needs in its item description or in the Compendium. After planting, use the watering item to hydrate the Soil. As long as the crop remains watered, it will continue to grow. If the crop isn't watered, its growth will stop, and it will start to rot. You can stop the rotting by watering it again, which will resume the growth process.<cf><cf>The time it takes for a crop to grow or rot, and the rate at which it consumes Water, depends on the specific plant. Crops also begin to rot if they are placed in the wrong Soil or if a potted plant is placed at an incorrect Altitude. However, crops in an Incubator don't have Altitude restrictions. You can find the required Altitude for a crop in the seedling's description or the Compendium.

E0468A8D4A79.text: To power devices on a Flying Ship, you need Energy, which is produced using Generators. To generate Energy, interact with a Generator, place a suitable Fuel source (wood, coal, etc.) into the Fuel Slot, and press "Start". The inserted Fuel will be consumed, producing Energy for a set duration. However, once the Generator is activated, Energy starts depleting immediately, regardless of whether any devices are connected. To stop Energy consumption, press "Stop" at any time.<cf><cf>Each Generator produces a fixed amount of Energy using Fuel. More advanced Generators can use higher-efficiency Fuels to produce more Energy or for longer periods.

D64B7021AB05.text: To properly construct a Flying Ship beyond your floating island with its Flying Ship Core, you need to create specific devices to control the Ship: Cockpit, Engine, Balloon, and Energy Generator.<cf><cf>The recipes for these components can be unlocked by interacting with Mechanisms found in shipwrecks on larger islands. Crafting these devices also requires unique materials that drop when you destroy the Ship's remains. Exploring shipwrecks is essential to gather both the knowledge and Resources needed to build your own functional Flying Ship.

4B71B821D38F.text: To swim, use the same movement keys as for walking. <cf>Swimming quickly consumes Stamina. <cf>While in Water, you cannot use Tools except for the Starter Compass. <cf><cf>Falling into deep Water from a height causes your Oxygen Bar to deplete if submerged, but diving isn't possible.<cf><cf>A Boat allows you to move faster across Water without using Stamina.<cf>To use a Boat, place it on the Water and enter it by pressing {InteractionAction}. <cf>Use {Move} to row forward/backward or rotate the Boat and turn left/right.<cf><cf>To exit the Boat, press {Jump}.

157F1992E306.text: To take control of a Flying Ship, a Cockpit must be placed on it. Stand in front of the Steering Wheel and use {InteractionAction} to interact with it to enter Captain Mode. <cf><cf>In Captain Mode you will control the Ship. <cf>Use {Move} to fly forward/backward and adjust speed or rotate the wheel and turn left/right.<cf>Use {FlyUpDown} to raise or lower your flying height. <cf>Press {ForceStopLever} to reset all adjustments and stop the Ship.<cf>Use {SwitchCamera} to switch between First Person Perspective and Flying Ship Perspective.<cf><cf>To exit Captain Mode use {QuitCockpit}.

2F009E1C40AA.text: To use a Boat, place it on the Water.<cf>Enter it by pressing {InteractionAction}.<cf>Use {Move} to row forward/backward or rotate the Boat.<cf>To exit the Boat, press {Jump}.

FB3312EF7BAC.text: Tools are essential for efficiently breaking Objects and Blocks. Different Tools are specialized for specific materials: Axes quickly chop down wooden Objects, Pickaxes break stone and mineral-based structures, while Shovels are best for loose materials like sand and dirt.<cf><cf>Each Tool has a Durability that decreases with use. Once it reaches zero, the Tool becomes broken and loses its functionality until repaired. Tools also vary in Efficiency, meaning higher-quality Tools perform their tasks faster.

75448FB58F70.text: Tools break Blocks faster when used on the right material (Axes-wood, Pickaxes-stone, Shovels-dirt). They have Durability that decreases with use, and higher-quality Tools work more efficiently.

BC8DCE625D8D.text: Use an Alchemy Station with Magical Liquid and Ingredients to craft Potions. Combine three Ingredients of the same magical aspect to create a basic Potion.

B9A4A9B2516E.text: Use movement keys to swim. While in Water, Tools can’t be used except for the Compass.

4776FA5C2717.text: Use the Block Crafting Station to craft Blocks. Select the Block and its variation, then press "Craft" if you have the required materials.

526CCB40EEBB.text: Use the Carpenter Station to craft furniture and Decorative Objects.

B2F3FCC8E3FA.text: Use the Cooking Station with Fuel and Ingredients to prepare Meals. Meals heal you and can provide other minor benefits.

62B6E7FE157A.text: Use the Crafting Station to create basic items from recipes. Advanced items like Weapons, Armor, and Mechanisms require specialized stations.

410095C980F4.text: Use the Rune Crafting Station to combine Runalit and five Fragments into a Rune, or five identical Runes into an upgraded one. Runes enhance Weapons and Chestplates with Special Effects.

50D86FEC8034.text: Use the Upgrade Station to enhance Equipment with Crystals of Force. Each Upgrade raises stats by one level (max 10). Costs increase at higher levels, but there is also a chance to add modifiers.

59ED8F62004C.text: Weapons have Damage values that determine how much Damage they deal during combat, as well as Durability, which indicates how long the Weapon will last before breaking.<cf><cf>Weapons also have additional stats: Shield and Shield Breaking (related to the weapon class). These stats determine the effectiveness of blocking while defending and the ability to penetrate an enemy's block when attacking.

4BEBB113C87F.text: While exploring the world and Dungeons, you may come across Locked Chests which contain better Loot than regular Chests. These Special Chests are secured with different types of locks, requiring either the correct Key or sufficient Lockpicking skills to open.<cf><cf>To unlock a Chest, hold a Key or Lockpick in your hand and interact with the Chest. Destroying a Locked Chest yields no Loot, so managing Keys and Lockpicks is essential for accessing valuable rewards hidden throughout the game world.

FF2C001694A2.text: While exploring the world, you can find Coins, the primary currency used for trading with Merchants. Merchants are special NPCs who offer players the ability to sell unwanted items and buy goods when interacted with. They are usually found in Villages.<cf><cf>Different Merchants offer different items for sale and buy items from the player at varying prices. This makes it worthwhile to seek out specific Merchants depending on what you wish to sell or purchase.

8F67DCBD5518.text: You have several key stats, including Health, Stamina, Defense, Oxygen, Experience, and Level. Managing these stats is essential for survival and progression.<cf><cf>Basic Health regeneration occurs automatically if your Health drops below 25%, but it will only restore up to 25% HP. To heal beyond this limit, you must consume Potions or Food. Automatic regeneration activates 5 seconds after taking no further Damage.<cf><cf>Stamina is consumed when performing actions like running, fast swimming, combat, or mining. It regenerates automatically and speeds up after 2.5 seconds of inactivity. Proper Stamina management is crucial for sustained exploration and combat efficiency.

00DE702A0A32.text: The Flying Ship is your primary method of travel as well as your mobile base. To make the Ship yours, place the Cockpit on the Ship type Island with a Flying Ship Core. This will set your Home Point and enable you to control the Ship's movement. Turn on Generators and connect them to Engines and Balloons to increase the maximum height and speed you can reach with your Flying Ship.
